checks.finding: All 13 requirements are atomic and observable. Each yields concrete, machine-checkable outputs: REQ-002 enumerates a closed per-artifact status set (match/mismatch/missing/too-large/path-denied/invalid-recorded-sha); REQ-003 defines verdict pass/fail from named invariants; REQ-004/005/006/007 give recompute-and-compare formulas with named result fields; REQ-008 asserts no-key/verified:false; REQ-009 pins host-script parity; REQ-010 byte-equal determinism; REQ-011 exception-safety; REQ-012/013 contract+test surfaces. No requirement rests on unmeasurable intent.

Each of the three goals maps to acceptance coverage. Goal 1 (read-only deterministic re-verify: disk-hash match + internal invariants) -> AC-001..AC-010, AC-013. Goal 2 (in-process implementation exactly matching host canonical artifacts-digest/spec_revision/git 40-hex formulas, signature/ancestry excluded) -> AC-012 host-script parity golden. Goal 3 (no-key-read / no-signature-verify boundary preserved) -> AC-011 no-secret + AC-014 static grep. No orphan goal.

All 16 ACs describe externally observable outcomes: verdict pass/fail, closed status enums, digest match/mismatch, echoed gitCommit fields with ancestryVerified:false, signature present/verified:false with canary-grep of response+stderr, byte-equal data, ajv contract conformance, and tools/list presence. Every AC carries a Requirement, Test ID, Test Type, and Test Target column; no vague-intent criteria.

Non-goals are explicit and enumerated (HMAC crypto verify, git HEAD/ancestry verify, bundle rewrite/repair/signing, contract self re-verification, new network/subprocess/fs-write, identity-ledger involvement). The primary expansion risk (git ancestry) is bounded to shape-only via OQ-001/ADR-0008 with a host-deferred report contract, coherently reflected in Non-goals, Edge Cases, REQ-006, and AC-008.

Material constraints are stated: no-exec/read-only boundary; path-guard allowlist (specs/, reports/, docs/review-tickets/, docs/workflow-improvements/, AGENTS.md) and key-file denylist; 2 MiB read cap; determinism (no time/random/network/process); v1 schema additive/backward-compatible (REQ-012); and the central security invariant (no key material read, no signature crypto verify) with a Security Boundaries table (B1/B2/B3). The security invariant is a clear, testable requirement (REQ-008 + AC-011/AC-014).

The three high-risk claims each have a planned validation surface: canonical-formula divergence from host (Risk: high) -> verbatim quote + golden parity AC-012; key-read leakage (Risk: high) -> denylist reuse + no-key AC-011 + static grep AC-014 (triple control); read exceptions on missing/oversize artifacts -> exception-safety AC-004/AC-005. No high-risk claim lacks a validation path, so SKIP does not apply.
